business.headline: Sensex ends 521 points higher, Nifty above 24,400; HDFC up 3%The BSE Sensex rose 521.16 points, or 0.67%, to close at 78,285.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 159.50 points, or 0.66%, to settle at 24,430.35.

Sebi plans to make it easier to short stocks. Here's what it means for investorsSebi is considering wider access to stock borrowing and lower collateral for short selling. The move is aimed at shifting activity towards cash equities as derivative losses mount for retail investors.

HDFC, RBL, ICICI jump up to 4%: Why are banking stocks rising today?HDFC Bank led a broad rally in banking stocks after reporting strong provisional June quarter business growth. The move lifted expectations for a solid earnings season even as analysts tracked loan mix, CASA pressure and improving macro signals.

How India kept fuel prices in check while the world burned through its walletsWhen the Strait of Hormuz shut down and crude surged past $120 a barrel, petrol prices rose 64% in Pakistan, 21% in Britain and 85% for diesel in the UAE. In Delhi, petrol went up 7.8% Here is how India managed that, and what it cost.
Apple restarts card payments for app store in India: ReportApple had stopped accepting credit and debit card payments in India in 2021 after the RBI introduced its card tokenisation mandate.

You may not be able to sell your electrical products from Oct 1. Here's whyIndia will require BIS certification and the ISI mark for 90 categories of electrical appliances from 1 October 2026. The phased rollout could disrupt manufacturing, imports and sales for businesses that miss approval timelines.

Can't activate your UAN on the EPFO portal? Here's why and what to do nowEPFO has ended UAN activation and direct UAN generation on its portal after the upgrade. The shift to UMANG with Aadhaar-based face authentication changes how PF subscribers access these services.

Made over Rs 1.25 lakh in equity gains? Here's why ITR-1 may not be enough this yearBefore filing your return, it is worth checking your capital gains statement carefully to ensure you choose the correct ITR form and avoid unnecessary notices or delays.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha talks end today. What has happened so far?The Bhubaneswar meetings, held on July 6 and 7, are part of the Commission's nationwide consultation exercise, where it is meeting employee unions, pensioner associations and other stakeholders before preparing its recommendations for the Centre.
Sensex opens 250 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HDFC Bank up 2%The BSE Sensex was up 0.33% at 78,020.50, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 0.33% to trade at 24,349.95 around 9:30 am.

Sitting on idle gold? Government may soon give you a reason to earn from itThe Centre may soon unveil a revamped Gold Monetisation Scheme that allows jewellers to collect household gold deposits. The proposed shift is aimed at drawing idle gold into the formal system and reducing reliance on imports.

Want to become a crorepati through SIP? Here's how much to invest and for how longBecoming a crorepati is no longer just a dream for the wealthy. With the right SIP strategy and enough time, you could get there too, but how much do you really need to invest each month?

The company powering Nvidia's AI chips wants a $28 billion Wall Street debutSK Hynix is set to launch a $28 billion US listing after a sharp rally driven by AI chip demand. The offering underlines how companies supplying the hardware behind artificial intelligence are drawing huge investor interest.

Maersk places fresh order for 1,000 India-made shipping containersThe announcement came as Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Sarbananda Sonowal, unveiled the first export-import (EXIM) shipping container manufactured in India for the company at the Maersk-CONCOR Inland Container Depot in UP's Dadri.

E20 even powers F1 cars: Auto experts dismiss concerns, back govt amid backlashIndustry experts defended the rollout of E20 fuel, saying extensive testing found no concerns in compatible vehicles. They said the ethanol push is tied to lower emissions, defined standards and reduced oil dependence.

Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

ITR 2026: Do you need to file two income tax returns? Tax department explainsThe new Income-tax Act has changed the way taxpayers talk about return filing, replacing "Assessment Year" with "Tax Year". But does that mean you'll have to file two ITRs? Here's what the tax department says.

Sensex, Nifty end higher on IT rally; HCLTech surges nearly 6%The BSE Sensex rose 261.79 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 77,763.91, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 95.15 points, or 0.39%, to close at 24,270.85.

IRDAI is finally going after the real reason behind insurance mis-sellingIRDAI is discussing a shift from upfront commissions to staggered payouts with the insurance industry. The proposed changes target mis-selling incentives and could tighten disclosure across distributors.

New EPFO Scheme: Will your employer contribute only Rs 1,800 to PF?The new EPFO Scheme, 2026 has raised an important question among salaried employees: will employers now limit their PF contribution to just Rs 1,800 a month? Here's what the new rules actually say.

entertainment.headline: Sikh body slams removal of Satluj, calls it attempt to suppress historical truth Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee president Harjinder Singh Dhami condemned the removal of Satluj from ZEE5 in India. He called it an attempt to suppress historical truth and demanded that the restrictions be lifted immediately.

No pro-Khalistan sentiment, shows truth: Khalra's lawyer on Satluj's OTT removal The takedown of Satluj after its brief Zee5 release has drawn sharply different reactions from people linked to the Jaswant Khalra case. While Advocate Navkiran Singh called the move wrong, former IAS officer KBS Sidhu said it may have been done to safeguard public order.

Aditya Dhar, Arjun Rampal praise force of nature Ranveer Singh on 41st birthday Director Aditya Dhar and actor Arjun Rampal marked actor Ranveer Singh's 41st birthday with unseen Dhurandhar pictures and personal notes. Their messages focused on his performance in the film and their shared bond forged on set.
ZEE5 breaks silence on Satluj takedown, urges viewers not to turn to piracy Remain hopeful of bringing film back, please don't support piracy: Zee5 on Satluj removal

Arjun Kapoor is happier than anyone else after sister Anshula's kaleera falls on him Actor Arjun Kapoor appeared happier than anyone else after sister Anshula Kapoor's kaleera fell on him during her chooda ceremony. Sharing the video, Anshula joked that she had never seen him so "committed" to getting chosen by a kaleera.

NTK leader seeks Tamil Nadu ban on Jr NTR-Trivikram film over Murugan row Seeman has asked for a ban on Jr NTR and Trivikram Srinivas's upcoming film in Tamil Nadu over its apparent portrayal of Lord Murugan. The demand follows backlash to the announcement line 'Born in the North' and has widened the debate over faith, identity and creative liberty.

Huma Qureshi dodges question on Toxic delay, says focus should be on August release Huma Qureshi brushed aside questions on the repeated postponements of Yash's Toxic and pointed to its new August 26 release date.

Diljit Dosanjh reacts as fan asks why Satluj chose OTT over theatres Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh has revealed why Satluj skipped a theatrical release and premiered directly on ZEE5, saying the team faced years of hurdles and was not even allowed to talk about the film before its release. The actor also reflected on the delays and thanked fans for supporting the project.

Satluj leaked online: Diljit Dosanjh's film available in HD a day after OTT removal Diljit Dosanjh's film Satluj is being widely shared online after its removal from Zee5. The circulation has intensified debate around censorship, free speech and the reach of digital distribution.

Satluj review: When a democracy forgets its dead, cinema remembersRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Satluj movie review: Diljit Dosanjh's film follows Jaswant Singh Khalra as he traces the disappeared through official records and cremation logs. The film turns that investigation into a stark question about memory, accountability and constitutional rights.

Pradeep Ranganathan turns producer, joins forces with Mamitha, Ashwath Marimuthu Actor-director Pradeep Ranganathan launched his home banner PR Show with PRS01, a multilingual film starring Mamitha Baiju and director Ashwath Marimuthu, while also marking his full-fledged debut as a producer.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan: Fake censor certificate sparks curiosity amid release buzz A fake CBFC certificate for CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan circulated online, claiming the film had received an A rating. However, it was soon pointed out that the censor certificate for Leo had been edited to make it appear as Jana Nayagan's.

Lock Upp 2's Yogesh Rawat reacts to Kangana Ranaut's cheater remark: It's traumatic Lock Upp 2 contestant Yogesh Rawat said he was hurt after Kangana Ranaut called him a "cheater" on the show. Speaking to Riteish Deshmukh, he claimed she did not know the full story and described the past six months as traumatic.

Exclusive: Actor Adivi Sesh to make Hindi debut, shoot begins in October Actor Adivi Sesh is set to make his Hindi film debut with an action drama scheduled to start filming in October. The project extends his reach beyond Telugu cinema as the makers prepare for an announcement soon.

Show Satluj to friends; I'm with Punjab till I die: Diljit after film's removal Diljit Dosanjh thanked viewers after Satluj was removed from Zee5 within two days of release. He said the film had still reached audiences and urged those who downloaded it to keep sharing it.

Diljit Dosanjh took a week off after Satluj: It stayed with me Actor Diljit Dosanjh's earlier comments on Satluj have resurfaced after ZEE5 removed the film in India. Dosanjh shared how he was emotioanlly drained while shooting for the film.

Big B thanks fans who braved Mumbai rains for Sunday darshan at Jalsa: Deeply moving Actor Amitabh Bachchan thanked fans for turning up outside Jalsa despite Mumbai's heavy rains and urged everyone to stay safe. His post comes after a viral video showed rainwater entering the compound of his iconic home - Jalsa.

Who was Jaswant Singh Khalra, the activist behind Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj? The removal of Diljit Dosanjh-starrer Satluj from Zee5 has renewed attention on social activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, whose life inspired the film. His work exposing alleged disappearances and secret cremations remains central to the story.

Explained: Satluj controversy, ban in India and link to Punjab insurgency Zee5 has removed Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj from its platform in India just days after releasing the film in its uncut version. Here's what happened.

general news.headline: Ram Mandir Trust's key meet on donation theft, chief Champat Rai not in attendanceThe Ram Mandir Trust has begun a crucial meeting amid the donation theft probe, but former general secretary Champat Rai is missing from the session. Will the trust take a call on key resignations and the SIT findings?

At Tehran's Azadi Square, Khamenei's ringed hand is new symbol of defianceMillions gathered in Tehran as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's coffin moved from Grand Mosalla to Azadi Square. A giant ringed-hand monument and delegations from nearly 100 countries underscored the funeral's symbolic and diplomatic weight.

E10 should continue or entire vehicle fleet…: Old Niti Aayog report flags concernThe six-year-old roadmap shows that while carmakers' body SIAM backed India's ethanol-blending programme, it also pushed for parallel E10 availability, arguing that existing vehicles would need protection-grade fuel during the transition to higher ethanol blends.

Satluj review: When a democracy forgets its dead, cinema remembersSatluj movie review: Diljit Dosanjh's film follows Jaswant Singh Khalra as he traces the disappeared through official records and cremation logs. The film turns that investigation into a stark question about memory, accountability and constitutional rights.

Why the Mumbai-Pune 'missing link' is so vulnerable to landslides, cavingHeavy monsoon rain triggered a landslide near Tunnel 2 and exposed surface defects on the newly opened Mumbai-Pune Expressway missing link. The disruption has turned the project's first monsoon into a key test of its slope stability, drainage and construction quality.

What was a hardcore BJP supporter doing at E20 petrol protest in Delhi?New Delhi's Jantar Mantar became the ground for the first protest against the Centre's decision to make the sale of 20% ethanol-blended (E20) petrol mandatory. Among the protesters were ardent BJP supporters and a person who said he had been linked to the RSS for decades.

Baruipur horror: How CCTV footage of girl's final hours led locals to suspectA CCTV clue led residents to a suspect after a 12-year-old girl went missing in Baruipur, near Kolkata. The suspect's alleged disclosure led to the recovery of her body from a pond, sparking widespread protests, a mob lynching and a political face-off.

13 dead in Maharashtra rain fury, Mumbai submerged, landslide buries Pune house Live UpdatesMumbai remained on high alert on Monday after the India Meteorological Department issued a red alert, warning of heavy to very heavy rainfall accompanied by strong winds.

Video: No headlights, Karnataka state bus navigates using mobile torchA viral video allegedly showed a KKRTC bus moving in darkness while its conductor used a mobile phone torch. The footage has intensified attacks on the Karnataka government over maintenance funds, delayed e-buses and unpaid reimbursements.

Mumbai wants a copy of NYC's Central Park. This might drown the city furtherAs Mumbai reels under another monsoon deluge, the city's plan to turn Mahalaxmi Racecourse into a Central Park-style public space is a matter that demands scrutiny. Experts have warned that redeveloping one of south Mumbai's largest natural flood buffers could make the city more vulnerable to waterlogging.

How Vijay is redefining Opposition politics: Target BJP, but tango with CentreFor C Joseph Vijay, opposing the BJP seemingly does not mean opposing the Centre. Even as he has positioned the BJP as his principal ideological enemy, the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister is pursuing a pragmatic working relationship with the Modi government, separating political battles from administrative priorities.

Live: Bridge collapses in Mahabaleshwar, stranded tourists evacuated with cranesMonsoon Tracker Live Updates: Heavy monsoon showers swept across several parts of the country on Sunday (July 5, 2026), with Mumbai witnessing severe disruption, while weather authorities issued alerts for Kerala, Odisha and Jharkhand. Delhi also experienced widespread rainfall as monsoon activity gathered pace. Mumbai bore the brunt of the spell after intense overnight rain led to the suspension of runway operations at the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport for about an hour. Official figures showed that several parts of the city received more than 200 mm of rainfall over the past 24 hours, while a few locations recorded nearly 300 mm. In eastern India, a well-marked low-pressure area over the Bay of Bengal strengthened into a depression, prompting Odisha to place all its districts on high alert. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the weather system is expected to cross the north Odisha coast between Chandbali and Digha within the next day, bringing heavy rainfall to Odisha and neighbouring West Bengal. Kerala also remained under the influence of active monsoon conditions, with widespread showers reported across most parts of the state. The IMD issued orange and yellow alerts for multiple districts. Meanwhile, Jharkhand has been placed under a yellow alert for thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across all 24 districts from July 6 to July 9. Stay tuned with indiatoday.in for all live updates.

116-year-old Karnataka woman treks to Tirumala on foot. CM Naidu reactsNavaneethamma, a 116-year-old woman from Karnataka, trekked to Tirumala on foot with her family. Her devotion drew attention from Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu.

Monsoon shifts gears: India records 11% surplus rain in first week of JulyThe southwest monsoon has revived sharply, pushing India into a rainfall surplus in early July. The active spell is expected to bring heavy rain to several states while supporting kharif sowing and reservoir levels.

A red card, 3 phone calls from Trump and a shocking FIFA U-turnFIFA found itself at the centre of a World Cup storm on Sunday after suspending Folarin Balogun's automatic red-card ban, allowing the United States striker to play against Belgium. The decision followed reported intervention from US President Donald Trump, who personally urged FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the case.

Row over retired Army officer's 'Many more to come' post on Aamir Khan's marriageA retired Lieutenant General's comment on Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt's wedding drew immediate scrutiny online. The exchange widened into a debate on X.

Abuse claims, 'how to die' searches surface in Delhi newlywed's deathAkriti Sutar, a 28-year-old newlywed, died after a fall at NDMC Flats in Lodhi Colony. Her family has alleged sustained abuse and murder, while police are examining digital searches suggesting possible suicidal intent.

Dear Neymar, thank you for teaching a generation what Joga Bonito looked likeFor millions who grew up in yellow and green, Neymar was never just Brazil's greatest player of a generation. He was our childhood, our impossible hope and our reason to believe that football could still be beautiful, even when it broke our hearts.

Mumbai-Pune Expressway 'missing link' shut 2 months after launch due to rain damageHeavy rain triggered a landslide near Tunnel 2 on the Missing Link, forcing an early diversion on the Pune-to-Mumbai carriageway. The closure pushed vehicles through Lonavala, worsening congestion and delaying commuters.

Bryan Johnson says his stomach is eating itself. Know about the diseaseBryan Johnson said tests and stomach biopsies confirmed he has autoimmune gastritis. The incurable condition has been linked to his long-running low iron levels and now requires ongoing monitoring.

Man finds rain-soaked diary on roadside, every page tells a heartwarming storyA man found a rain-soaked diary found on a roadside and appealed for help tracing its owner. The handwritten keepsake stirred viewers, who hoped the damaged memories could still be recovered.

sports.headline: Unjustifiable: European football body slams Donald Trump's red card intervention FIFA World Cup 2026: European football's governing body has blasted the decision to lift United States striker Folarin Balogun's mandatory suspension for the Belgium clash, warning that political intervention directly threatens the integrity of the game.

World Cup: Ex-FIFA chief slams Trump-linked red card reversal for US striker Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter has slammed FIFA after the governing body controversially suspended Folarin Balogun's automatic one-match ban, allowing the United States striker to face Belgium in Monday's World Cup Round of 16 clash.

Tearful Casemiro comforted by journalists after Brazil's painful World Cup exit Casemiro struggled to hold back tears as he reflected on Brazil's heartbreaking exit from the FIFA World Cup after a stunning 2-1 defeat to the Norwegian national football team in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Mexico boss Aguirre steps down after World Cup exit, backs Marquez as successor Javier Aguirre's third spell as Mexico coach ended on Sunday after the co-hosts suffered a 3-2 defeat to England in the World Cup Round of 16, with the veteran manager backing assistant Rafa Marquez to succeed him.

My wife is Argentine: Cristiano Ronaldo reveals funny chat with flight attendant Cristiano Ronaldo recalled a funny exchange with an Argentine flight attendant before Portugal's Spain clash. The anecdote briefly eased a press conference dominated by questions over age, form and retirement.

Freak celebration injury! Jordan Henderson rushed to hospital after England win FIFA World Cup: England midfielder Jordan Henderson was rushed to hospital after suffering a serious wrist injury during the post-match celebrations following his side's dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Dear Neymar, thank you for teaching a generation what Joga Bonito looked like For millions who grew up in yellow and green, Neymar was never just Brazil's greatest player of a generation. He was our childhood, our impossible hope and our reason to believe that football could still be beautiful, even when it broke our hearts.

The decision followed reported intervention from US President Donald Trump, who personally urged FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the case.

I've lost my voice: Harry Kane revels after England oust Mexico in Azteca epic Harry Kane could barely speak after England's thrilling 3-2 win over Mexico in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16, admitting he had "lost his voice" after celebrating a famous victory with teammates and travelling supporters at the Azteca Stadium.

Watch: Ochoa retires in tears as England end Mexico's World Cup dream Guillermo Ochoa's storied career ended in heartbreak on Sunday as the legendary goalkeeper retired after Mexico's 3-2 defeat to England in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.

England survive the fires of Azteca to end Mexico's World Cup dream England survived a dramatic Azteca thriller to beat co-hosts Mexico 3-2 and book their place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Thomas Tuchel's side overcame a hostile atmosphere, controversy and relentless pressure to keep their title dream alive.

Why Bruno Guimaraes took Brazil's penalty over Vini Jr vs Norway? Ancelotti explainsCarlo Ancelotti revealed Bruno Guimaraes was Brazil's third-choice penalty taker against Norway after Neymar and Raphinha were unavailable. The Newcastle midfielder's miss proved costly as Brazil crashed out of the FIFA World Cup.

Neymar retires from international football after Brazil's World Cup exitFIFA World Cup: Neymar has retired from international football after Brazil's shock Round of 16 exit to Norway at the World Cup. The Selecao legend announced his decision moments after the defeat, ending a glittering 16-year career as Brazil's record goalscorer.

MEX 2-3 ENG Highlights: England knock Mexico out of World CupMexico (Mexico) vs England (ENG) Highlights, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: England have booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals with a hard-fought 3-2 victory over Mexico. After racing into a commanding lead, England survived relentless late pressure with a determined defensive display to end Mexico's World Cup campaign.

Erling Haaland ends Brazil's sleepy dream of a sixth World Cup with just 2 shotsCarlo Ancelotti built a Brazil that refused to lose control. Against Norway, they controlled so little of the game that Erling Haaland only needed two chances to end their dreams of a sixth FIFA World Cup title.

Neymar sobs uncontrollably after Brazil's shock World Cup exit vs Norway: WatchNeymar was left inconsolable after Brazil's shock 2-1 defeat to Norway ended their FIFA World Cup campaign. The veteran forward broke down in tears following the final whistle, with footage capturing the heartbreak of another painful exit.

Jannik Sinner scripts another slice of history, sweeps into Wimbledon quartersDefending champion Jannik Sinner stormed into his fifth consecutive Wimbledon quarterfinal with a dominant straight-sets win over Shintaro Mochizuki, extending his winning streak at the All England Club to 11.

Cristiano Ronaldo confirms he's playing his last World CupCristiano Ronaldo confirmed the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the last of his illustrious career, saying he wants to savour every moment of the tournament. The Portugal captain also expressed hope that his side's campaign against Spain will not be his final match.

Sabalenka vents frustration after Wimbledon exit: Want to get completely drunkAryna Sabalenka admitted she wanted to get completely drunk after her Wimbledon title hopes ended with a straight-sets defeat to Naomi Osaka, who outplayed the World No. 1 to reach her maiden quarter-final at the All England Club.

BRA 1-2 NOR Highlights: Haaland leads Norway to knock Brazil out in R16Brazil (BRA) vs Norway (NOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (BRA 1-2 NOR) Norway knock Brazil out of the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a famous victory. Erling Haaland's brace and Orjan Nyland's heroics seal a quarter-final spot, while Neymar's late penalty proves only a consolation for the Selecao.

Aryna Sabalenka crashes out as Naomi Osaka reaches maiden Wimbledon quartersNaomi Osaka defeated World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka 6-2, 7-6(2) in a statement Centre Court display on Sunday to reach her maiden Wimbledon quarter-final.

Ruthless Australia blow away England, clinch 7th Women's T20 World Cup titleAustralia produced a ruthless, dominant performance to crush England and win a record-extending seventh Women's T20 World Cup title with a seven-wicket victory at Lord's Cricket Ground.

Great injustice reversed: Trump on FIFA's shocking reversal of US striker's banFIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump welcomed FIFA's decision to suspend the implementation of Folarin Balogun's match ban, clearing the USA striker to face Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16.

Novak Djokovic breaks Roger Federer's Wimbledon record, reaches quarter-finalsNovak Djokovic battled past Roman Safiullin to reach the Wimbledon 2026 quarter-finals, registering a record 106th men's singles win at SW19 to surpass Roger Federer. Jessica Pegula and Karolina Muchova also advanced to the last eight.

Watch: Nat Sciver-Brunt, baby Theo create unforgettable T20 World Cup final momentEngland captain Nat Sciver-Brunt melted hearts by walking onto the Lord's field with her infant son Theo before the Women's T20 World Cup final, showing the growing acceptance of motherhood in elite cricket.

Lewis Hamilton takes British GP podium in P3 after late drama, Leclerc winsFormula 1: Lewis Hamilton finished third for Ferrari at the British Grand Prix in a race defined by late safety car drama and strategic calls that reshaped the podium. Charles Leclerc won the race, while George Russell took second as late incidents, including Max Verstappen's crash, neutralised the final stages.

Why Cabo Verde's 51st Independence day felt extra special this yearFIFA World Cup 2026: Cabo Verde returned home to packed streets and a thunderous airport welcome after their World Cup exit. The reception on the nation's 51st Independence Day showed how this run had become a marker of national pride.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for 2027 World Cup? Ex-spinner makes bold ODI predictionL Sivaramakrishnan has urged India to consider Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for the 2027 World Cup. The former spinner said the teenager should be backed in white-ball cricket as a long-term match-winner.

Day 7 Highlights: Sinner beats Mochizuki, advances to quarterfinalsWimbledon Day 7 Live Score: Jannik Sinner beat Japan's Shintaro Mochizuki and advanced to the quarterfinals. Earlier, Novak Djokovic beat Roman Safiullin on Centre Court and advanced to the quarters. Aryna Sabalenka has been knocked out after losing to Naomi Osaka.

Not Cape Verde hero, Real Madrid sign Dutch star in right-back positionReal Madrid have completed the signing of Netherlands international Denzel Dumfries from Inter Milan, ending speculation over their right-back search. This brings a momentary end to Sidny Lopez Cabral dream of playing under Jose Mourinho for the second time in his career.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
